concerns.text: Constitution Principle I requires 'Random seeds MUST be pinned in code/' but the spec contains no mention of random seed pinning anywhere. This is a missing requirement that conflicts with the Constitution.
Constitution Principle III requires 'Datasets MUST be checksummed' but the spec mentions filtering/preprocessing without explicitly requiring dataset checksumming. This is a missing requirement.
Constitution Principle VII requires 'test statistic, p-value, and effect size recorded in results/' but the spec only mentions p-values. Effect size recording is missing from the spec requirements.

Edge Cases state 'function length distributions differ by >30% after filtering' but FR-002 specifies 'medians within ±20%'. The 30% threshold should be clarified as a failure condition when the 20% target cannot be achieved, to avoid confusion.

FR-008 requires sensitivity analysis across {0.01, 0.05, 0.1} but references '(See US-3)' while User Story 3 does not explicitly mention this sensitivity analysis requirement. SC-005 does reference it, creating partial but incomplete traceability.
Title uses 'LLM-Assisted Metrics' but the spec content consistently uses 'LLM-generated code'. Consider unifying terminology to 'LLM-generated code metrics' for consistency.

FR-001 references '(See US-1)' but User Story 1 specifies '≥1000 Python snippets per group' while FR-001 does not mention this count requirement. Consider adding the count to FR-001 for complete traceability.
